Black Mass (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $62,575,678Details
International Box Office $37,400,000
Worldwide Box Office $99,975,678
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,526,542
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,268,824
Total $11,795,366
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In 1970s South Boston, FBI Agent John Connolly persuades Irish mobster James "Whitey" Bulger to collaborate with the FBI and eliminate a common enemy: the Italian mob. The drama tells the story of this unholy alliance, which spiraled out of control, allowing Whitey to evade law enforcement, consolidate power, and become one of the most ruthless and powerful gangsters in Boston history.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$22,635,037 (36.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.76 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:62.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$53,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,188 opening theaters/3,188 max. theaters, 4.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $88,778,781

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 18th, 2015 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: September 17th, 2015 (Wide) (Singapore)
September 17th, 2015 (Wide) (Central America)
September 18th, 2015 (Wide) (India)
September 18th, 2015 (Wide) (Indonesia)
September 24th, 2015 (Wide) (Bolivia)
Video Release: January 12th, 2016 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for brutal violence, language throughout, some sexual references and brief drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2359, 2/4/2015)
Running Time: 122 minutes
Geographical setting: Boston
Plot point: Biography, Confidential Informant, FBI, Hitmen, Mafia, True Crime
Social setting: Organized Crime
Subgenre: Crime Drama
Time period setting: 1970s, 1980s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., Cross Creek Pictures, Le Grisbi, Free State Pictures, Head Gear Films, RatPac Entertainment, Dune Entertainment, Vendian Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

In 1970s South Boston, FBI Agent John Connolly persuades Irish mobster James "Whitey" Bulger to collaborate with the FBI and eliminate a common enemy: the Italian mob. The drama tells the story of this unholy alliance, which spiraled out of control, allowing Whitey to evade law enforcement, consolidate power, and become one of the most ruthless and powerful gangsters in Boston history.
